name: Leeward Bowl
tagline: Old-school Pearl City bowling alley with 28 lanes, a bar & grill, and league nights — where local families have bowled for decades.
parking: Shopping center lot on Kamehameha Hwy
bestTime: Weekday afternoons for open lanes; league nights for local atmosphere
talkStory: Leeward Bowl is the kind of place that doesn't need to be trendy to survive — it just needs to keep the lanes waxed and the beer cold. Twenty-eight synthetic lanes with automatic scoring, bumper guards for kids, and the eight50 Bar & Grill serving shoyu chicken and local-style pub food. Thursday league nights bring out the Pearl City regulars who've been bowling here for years. It also runs the state's largest Junior Bowling program, so expect family energy on weekends. Not polished, not trying to be — just a real local hangout where the entertainment is the point.
